extraction.one_line_summary: RUBIO Einer won shortened Stage 13 (75km, single climb) after PINOT Thibaut animated with repeated attacks but got out-sprinted by 6 seconds.
tactical_patterns: PINOT made multiple attacks on final climb but burned matches for nothing, losing sprint to wheel-sucker RUBIO by 6 seconds.
CEPEDA Jefferson Alexander stayed in break despite teammate CARTHY attacking behind, tactical conflict between EF riders split effort.
FORTUNATO Lorenzo first GC attack neutralized immediately, suggesting peloton controlled tempo despite aggressive breakaway.
SWIFT Ben paced valley for GC group exceptionally well, domestique signal on shortened stage suggests INEOS depth on modified routes.

rider_signals.RUBIO Einer: positive: Won from break after sitting on PINOT's repeated attacks, matured tactically to take calculated sprint.
rider_signals.PINOT Thibaut: negative: Attacked excessively on final climb without distancing anyone, wasted energy for second place in supposed final GT.
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: neutral: Attacked outside top-10 to gain time but couldn't follow when CEPEDA went, coordination issue with teammate in break.
rider_signals.SWIFT Ben: positive: Paced GC group uphill/downhill/flat on shortened stage, exceptional domestique performance in extreme weather protocol.
rider_signals.LEKNESSUND Andreas: negative: Lost 7 seconds on shortened single-climb stage, concerning weakness signal for Maglia Rosa defense.
rider_signals.GEE Derek: positive: Another top-5 from break, consistently animating stages suggesting breakthrough GT form.
rider_signals.CEPEDA Jefferson Alexander: neutral: Active in break but fell out tactically with PINOT's attacks, team tactics unclear with CARTHY behind.
